commands.review_summary:
The PR teaches `stageSandboxMedia` to resolve managed `media://inbound/*` references into runner-readable staged files and adds sandbox and host-mode regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +64. Total +107 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main renders managed inbound documents as `media://inbound` prompt refs and only inlines images for current-turn attachments. I did not rerun the live Telegram reproduction, but the PR body includes before-baseline Crabbox proof.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainer/owner review should decide the security-sensitive host-mode staging contract before this active PR lands.

target.title: fix: make inbound document attachments readable by runners
target.body:
Closes #97237

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users sending non-image files, such as PDFs, over Telegram would have the attachment stored successfully but the agent would only see an unresolvable `media://inbound/...` prompt reference. Shell-based readers and custom document readers could not open that URI, so the agent could report that no usable attachment path was available.

## Why This Change Was Made

This extends the existing inbound media staging owner to resolve managed `media://inbound/*` references before agent execution, instead of adding a Telegram-specific prompt workaround. Managed local media is copied through the existing safe staging boundary, sandboxed runs keep runner-relative `media/inbound/...` paths, host-mode runs use an isolated per-turn workspace path to avoid clobbering project files, and remote media cache behavior is unchanged.

## User Impact

Agents can now receive a live Telegram PDF/document attachment and see a filesystem path that their normal runner tools can open. The managed inbound media store remains the source of truth, and host-mode staging avoids deterministic writes over existing workspace files.

## Evidence

AI-assisted change; reviewed and validated before opening.

- `$autoreview`: clean after addressing two accepted findings about host-mode clobbering and unanchored relative paths.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.trigger-handling.stages-inbound-media-into-sandbox-workspace.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/media-note.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.media-note.test.ts` passed: 32 tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Crabbox live proof: provider `aws`, run `run_0f701937db9a`, lease `cbx_4c6768b0f6a5` (`golden-crayfish`). The run built the branch, leased only Convex `kind=telegram`, sent the W3C dummy PDF to the live Telegram SUT, and verified the mock-model prompt had zero `media://inbound` refs plus a staged PDF path whose file existed with size `13264` bytes. Artifact: `.crabbox/runs/run_0f701937db9a/run_0f701937db9a-artifacts.tgz`.
- Regression baseline before this change: Crabbox provider `aws`, run `run_3c24f0d166a1`, lease `cbx_8e6be2a363ae`, reproduced the old prompt containing `[media attached: media://inbound/dummy---...pdf (application/pdf)]` while the managed inbound file existed.
target.author: joshavant
